conversation.content: Hey Aru! It's been a while since we talked. How are things?

Things are going great since my ACL is now fully healed and I'm back to playing basketball. I'm busy leading the robotics club's software team and getting ready for my SAIL internship starting in June!
That's such a relief about your ACL—have you finally been able to start bouldering like you wanted? I'm also so hyped for your SAIL internship and that upcoming Tokyo trip!

I'm loving bouldering at the campus wall now that my ACL is fully healed and I'm officially off all medications. I've already booked my flights for Tokyo in August and am getting ready to start at SAIL next month!
It's awesome that you're totally off the meds and already scaling the campus wall! What's the first thing on your itinerary once you land in Tokyo this August?

I'm planning to hit up some local sushi spots and explore the tech districts as soon as we settle into our Airbnb. I'm also really excited to check out some of Tokyo's famous bouldering gyms while I'm there!
Tokyo bouldering gyms are legendary, so you’ll have to tell me how the grading compares to the campus wall! Are you doing any specific prep for the SAIL internship before it kicks off in June?

I'm prepping by leading the robotics club's software team and reading computer vision papers for my June start at SAIL. Since my ACL is fully healed, I’m also getting in lots of bouldering practice now that I've quit guitar!
Those CV papers sound intense, but I bet leading the robotics software team is great practice for SAIL. How does it feel trading in the guitar for bouldering chalk now that your knee is back to 100%?
It feels amazing to be active again since I quit guitar and my ACL is fully healed. Bouldering is a much better fit for my schedule now that I'm back to 100% and leading the robotics software team.

It's awesome that you've found a better rhythm with bouldering and the robotics club. Since you're leading the software team, do you think any of the projects you're working on will carry over to your SAIL research?

The perception work I lead in the robotics club definitely overlaps with the computer vision research I'll start at SAIL this June. It's great to be fully focused on these projects now that my ACL is healed and I've traded guitar for bouldering.

That overlap sounds like it'll give you a huge head start when you kick things off at SAIL next month! Have you already scouted which specific Tokyo neighborhoods you want to go bouldering in this August?

I've been looking at gyms in Akihabara and Setagaya for our August trip since my ACL is fully healed and I'm bouldering constantly. It’s been great having more time for it since I quit guitar and started leading the robotics software team!
Akihabara is going to be such a vibe for both the tech and the climbing! Since you're deep in those CV papers, have you found any specific algorithms you're dying to test out once you start at SAIL?

I'm really excited to dive into Vision Transformers for robotic manipulation when I start at SAIL in June. It's much easier to focus on research and leading the robotics team now that my ACL is fully healed and I’ve swapped guitar for bouldering!

Vision Transformers are a total game-changer for manipulation, so you're definitely going to have an incredible summer at SAIL! Have you checked if any of those Tokyo bouldering gyms have high-tech LED walls or moonboards to try out?
I'm definitely looking for gyms with Moonboards to test my strength now that my ACL is fully healed and I've quit guitar. It’s the perfect way to stay active before my SAIL internship starts in June!

Moonboards in Tokyo sound like the perfect challenge for your newly healed knee! Are you planning to wrap up any big milestones with the robotics software team before SAIL starts taking over your life in June?

We're finishing our latest perception stack for the robotics club before I start at SAIL in June. My ACL is fully healed and I'm totally healthy, so I've been putting all my extra energy into leading the team and bouldering!

It’s awesome that the perception stack is wrapping up right as you're feeling 100% again! Are you planning any big celebratory bouldering sessions or maybe a Tokyo-themed dinner before the SAIL internship starts in June?

Jake and I are planning a celebratory dinner with plenty of salmon and chicken before my SAIL internship starts in June. Since my ACL is fully healed and I've quit guitar, I’m also hitting the bouldering wall extra hard!

That dinner with Jake sounds like the perfect way to fuel up before SAIL kicks off in June! Since you're bouldering so much now, are you planning to bring your own shoes to Tokyo or just rent them at those gyms?

I’ll definitely bring my own shoes to Tokyo in August since my ACL is fully healed and I’ve quit guitar to focus on bouldering. I am completely healthy and off all meds as I prepare for my SAIL internship starting in June!

That’s definitely the move—nothing beats your own shoes when you're tackling those tough Tokyo problems! Do you have a specific bouldering grade you're aiming to hit before your SAIL internship starts next month?

I'm aiming for V5 now that my ACL is fully healed and I'm off all medications. It's a great goal to reach before my SAIL internship starts in June, especially since I've quit guitar to focus entirely on bouldering!

Hitting V5 before June is a killer goal, especially now that you're finally feeling 100% and back in action! Do you think you’ll celebrate hitting that milestone with some new gear for your Tokyo bouldering spree?

I might pick up some new bouldering gear for Tokyo since my ACL is fully healed and I've quit guitar to focus on climbing. I'm completely healthy and ready to hit my V5 goal before starting at SAIL this June!

New gear would be a great reward for hitting V5 before you head off to SAIL in June! Since you're leading the robotics team, are you doing anything special to hand off the perception stack before the internship starts?

I'm training the next software lead and documenting our perception stack before I start at SAIL in June. My ACL is fully healed and I'm feeling 100%, so I'm putting all my energy into the club and bouldering!
Documenting that stack is a pro move before you dive into SAIL next month! Since you're finally at 100%, are you planning to hit any outdoor bouldering spots before the summer heat really kicks in?

I'm planning to hit Castle Rock soon since my ACL is fully healed and I'm completely healthy now. It's my main focus along with the robotics club before my SAIL internship starts in June, especially since I quit guitar!

Since you’ve been so focused on bouldering and leading the robotics team, do you ever find yourself missing the guitar at all? Or are you just enjoying having all that extra time and energy for your SAIL prep?

I don't miss guitar at all because I'm focused on bouldering and leading the robotics team now that my ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy. Having that extra energy is perfect as I prepare to start my SAIL internship this June!

It's awesome that you're so dialed into bouldering and SAIL that you don't even miss the guitar! Do you think the endurance you're building at the wall will help with those long days exploring Tokyo in August?

Bouldering definitely builds the endurance I'll need for Tokyo since my ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy. It’s been great focusing on climbing and the robotics team since I quit guitar and started prepping for my SAIL internship this June!

That endurance will definitely come in handy for navigating the busy streets of Tokyo! Have you heard which specific research project or mentor you'll be paired with at SAIL when you start next month?

I'll be working under Prof. Fei-Fei Li on computer vision for robotics when I start at SAIL in June. Since my ACL is fully healed and I've quit guitar, I'm feeling 100% and ready to dive into research while continuing to boulder!

Prof. Fei-Fei Li is an absolute legend, so working with her at SAIL is going to be an incredible experience! Since you're finally at 100% and hitting V5s, are you feeling like this is going to be your best summer yet?

This will definitely be my best summer yet since my ACL is fully healed and I'm starting my SAIL internship under Prof. Fei-Fei Li in June. I'm feeling 100% healthy as I focus on bouldering and get ready for my Tokyo trip in August!

It’s seriously the ultimate summer—interning for a legend at SAIL and then celebrating in Tokyo! Are you planning to look for any specialized robotics or AI exhibits while you’re exploring the tech districts in August?

I'm definitely checking out robotics exhibits in Akihabara since I lead the software team and start at SAIL this June. My ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy, so I'm focusing on bouldering now that I've quit guitar!

Akihabara is the perfect place to nerd out after a summer at SAIL, and it's great you'll be able to explore it all on a fully healed knee! Since you've swapped guitar for bouldering, are you planning to visit any of those specialized climbing gear shops while you're in Tokyo?

I'm definitely planning to visit specialized bouldering shops in Tokyo since my ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy. I've quit guitar to focus on climbing and I'm excited to explore some gear before my SAIL internship starts in June!

Tokyo has some of the coolest climbing gear you can't find anywhere else, so you’ll definitely come back with some awesome stuff! Since June is right around the corner, do you have any final goals for the robotics team before you transition into SAIL?

I'm focusing on completing our latest perception stack and training my replacement before I start at SAIL in June. Since my ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy, I've had so much energy for leading the robotics team and bouldering now that I've quit guitar!

Training your replacement is a solid move to make sure the robotics club stays strong while you're at SAIL in June! Do you think you'll try to find a bouldering gym closer to the lab to save on commute time?

I'll definitely be looking for a bouldering gym near SAIL since my ACL is fully healed and I've quit guitar to focus on climbing. It's much easier to plan my schedule for the June internship now that I'm 100% healthy and off all meds!

That sounds like the perfect way to keep your V5 progress going while you're deep in research at SAIL! Have you checked if there are any local climbing meetups near the lab to make some new friends before you head to Tokyo?

I'm definitely checking for local climbing meetups near SAIL since my ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy. It's much easier to focus on bouldering and leading the robotics team now that I've quit guitar and I'm starting my internship in June!

Meeting other climbers at SAIL will be a great way to network while staying active before your big Tokyo trip in August! Since you're training your replacement now, do you think they'll be able to keep up with the high standards you set for the robotics team?

I'm training them to keep our perception stack in check and lead the software team after I start at SAIL this June. My ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy, so I've been putting all my extra energy into bouldering since I quit guitar!

You're leaving the robotics team in great hands while you head to SAIL next month! Since you've got so much energy for bouldering, are you planning a final celebratory climb with the whole team before you start your internship?

I'm planning a final bouldering session with the robotics software team before I start at SAIL in June. My ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy, so I've been putting all my extra energy into climbing now that I've quit guitar!
That team bouldering session sounds like an epic way to close this chapter before you head to SAIL in June! Are you planning to show off some of those V5 moves to the rest of the robotics crew?

I'm definitely planning to show off some V5 moves during our final session since my ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy. It's been great focusing on bouldering and leading the robotics team since I quit guitar and I'm starting at SAIL in June!

They’re going to be so impressed by those V5 moves, especially knowing how hard you worked to recover from that ACL injury! Since you're heading to SAIL next month, have you thought about how you'll balance the research with your final Tokyo trip prep?

I'm balancing my SAIL internship prep with bouldering and planning my August trip to Tokyo. My ACL is fully healed and I'm 100% healthy, so I've been putting all my extra energy into leading the robotics team now that I've quit guitar!
It's incredible that your knee is back to 100% just in time to tackle those Tokyo bouldering gyms this August! Have you decided which neighborhood you're staying in for the trip?
